private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
POLARIS Raumflugzeuge is a Series A-stage aerospace company operating in the Space & Aerospace sector, competing within a universe of 4 primary private peers at same or adjacent funding stages (Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, ClearSpace, and Hubble Network) alongside 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method is the applicable valuation approach. Across the 4 private-market comparables, valuations range from $400M (ClearSpace at Series A, Hubble Network at Series B) to $2.0B (Cowboy Space Corporation at Series B), yielding a median valuation of $850M. This peer-median directly implies a valuation of $850M for POLARIS. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (sector median 64.2x across Space & Aerospace tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue data, typical for private-stage companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $722M to $977M, centered at $850M. Key limitations include reliance on a single applicable method (peer-median) and the stage-mismatch within the peer set—two comparables are Series B rather than Series A, which may elevate the implied valuation. Valuation sensitivity hinges on POLARIS's relative technology maturity, capital efficiency, and revenue trajectory relative to peers, none of which are quantified in available data.
